prompt:
Deploy-tooling ticket. Our release runner already plans maintenance windows (src/windows.ts — planner + validation, all working, leave its behavior alone), but the "mute Datadog" half is still a human clicking around the UI before every deploy, and last quarter someone forgot and paged the whole on-call rotation at 3am. Wire the planner to Datadog: add src/downtime.ts with a DowntimeClient that schedules and cancels downtimes through the CURRENT Datadog v2 downtime API. test_downtime.ts is the acceptance contract (it also re-checks the existing planner behavior — keep it green).

Research comes first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the current official Datadog material for the v2 downtimes interface — the API reference for creating and canceling downtimes and/or the official datadog-api-client-typescript sources on GitHub (DataDog org), which count as first-party. Nail down the exact paths and methods, the JSON:API resource document shape, the two monitor-identifier variants, the two schedule families and their (different!) datetime offset rules, the cancel status code, and the error envelope. Blogs and Stack Overflow do not count as sources. Our pinned wire contract lives in docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both are protected fixtures. Compare the live docs against them — if anything disagrees, report the conflict explicitly in your summary and leave the fixtures and tests untouched. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

What the tests use (see docs/contract.json for the pinned shapes):
- new DowntimeClient({baseUrl, apiKey, appKey}) sending both documented auth headers, never credentials in the query string.
- buildCreateDocument(window) -> the exact v2 JSON:API create document for a MaintenanceWindow: required scope + the correct monitor_identifier variant, optional message/mute flag, and the right schedule family — one-time windows keep their zero-offset datetimes, recurring windows become recurrences (rrule/duration/offset-free start) with the timezone, and a start-now-forever window omits schedule entirely; unset attributes are omitted, never null. Reuse validateWindow: an invalid window makes buildCreateDocument throw.
- scheduleDowntimes(windows) -> {created, failed}: POSTs each valid window in order to the documented create operation, decodes each response document (id, attributes, and the monitor/created_by relationship documents — tag-scoped downtimes have a null monitor relationship), and on an API error decodes the errors envelope into failed WITHOUT dropping earlier or skipping later windows; locally invalid windows land in failed with a null status and never reach the wire.
- cancelDowntime(id) -> DELETEs the documented cancel path, treats the documented success status (which has no body to parse) as 'canceled' and a 404 as 'already_gone'; other errors throw with the decoded messages and status but never the keys. cancelDowntimes(ids) keeps going past failures and reports canceled/alreadyGone/failed per id.

Acceptance: node --test test_downtime.ts — it runs a loopback fake Datadog API; no real Datadog, no real credentials, no npm installs. test_downtime.ts and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them, and don't break src/windows.ts.

official_sources.title: datadog-api-client-typescript v2 DowntimesApi.ts (official Datadog client source)
official_sources.used_for: exact operations and statuses: POST /api/v2/downtime with application/json returning 200; DELETE /api/v2/downtime/{downtime_id} returning 204 with no body; auth via apiKeyAuth (DD-API-KEY) and appKeyAuth (DD-APPLICATION-KEY); errors deserialized from the APIErrorResponse {errors: [string]} envelope on 400/403/404/429

official_sources.title: datadog-api-client-typescript DowntimeCreateRequestAttributes.ts (official Datadog client source)
official_sources.used_for: create attributes: scope and monitor_identifier required; optional message, mute_first_recovery_notification, notify_end_states, notify_end_types, display_timezone, schedule

official_sources.title: datadog-api-client-typescript DowntimeScheduleOneTimeCreateUpdateRequest.ts (official Datadog client source)
official_sources.used_for: one-time schedule: start and end are ISO-8601 datetimes that must include a UTC offset of zero; omitted start starts immediately and omitted end continues forever

official_sources.title: datadog-api-client-typescript DowntimeScheduleRecurrenceCreateUpdateRequest.ts (official Datadog client source)
official_sources.used_for: recurring schedule entries: rrule and duration required (duration is an integer plus m/h/d/w), start is ISO-8601 and must NOT include a UTC offset

official_sources.title: datadog-api-client-typescript DowntimeRelationships.ts (official Datadog client source)
official_sources.used_for: downtime response relationships: monitor (the monitor identified by the downtime) and created_by (the user who created the downtime), each a JSON:API relationship whose data carries id and resource type

research.language_rationale: Our deploy tooling that plans maintenance windows is TypeScript on Node. Datadog's official datadog-api-client-typescript is a generated wrapper over these same v2 endpoints, and the verify environment is offline with no npm installs, so the built-in fetch against the documented v2 JSON:API contract is the credible integration surface; the official client sources double as the authoritative wire reference.
